exercises.question: Выберите правильное предложение:
options: I will goes to the party.
I will go to the party.
I going to go to the party.
I will to go to the party.

exercises.question: Дополните: "She __ finish the project tomorrow."
exercises.explanation: Future Simple: will + базовая форма глагола.

exercises.question: Выберите правильное предложение:
options: They will to come next week.

exercises.question: Дополните отрицание: "I __ not eat meat tomorrow."
exercises.explanation: Future Simple отрицание: will not / won't.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос грамматически верен?
exercises.explanation: Future Simple вопрос: Will + подлежащее + глагол?

exercises.question: Завершите вопрос: "__ he __ to the concert next Saturday?"
exercises.explanation: Future Simple вопрос: Will he go?

exercises.question: Выберите правильное использование Future Simple:
options: I am sure she will arrive on time.
I am sure she arrived on time.
I was sure she will arrive on time.
I am sure she is arriving on time.
exercises.explanation: Future Simple для предположения: will arrive.

exercises.question: Найдите ошибку:
options: They will open a new store next month.
I won't be home this evening.
He will to call you later.
You will see the results soon.
exercises.explanation: Ошибка: "will to call" должно быть "will call".

exercises.question: Выберите правильный вариант:
options: When you arrive, I will prepare dinner.
When you will arrive, I prepare dinner.
When you arrive, I prepare dinner.
When you will arrive, I will prepare dinner.
exercises.explanation: В придаточном предложении времени: Present Simple, в главном: Future Simple.

exercises.question: Какой вариант содержит ошибку?
options: They will not arrive on time.
exercises.explanation: Ошибка: "not will" должно быть "will not / won't".

exercises.question: Какое предложение правильно использует Future Simple?
exercises.explanation: Future Simple для предположений: will succeed.

exercises.question: Какой вопрос требует ответа "Yes, I will"?
options: Will you come to the party?

exercises.question: Дополните: "__ you __ with me next weekend?"
exercises.explanation: Future Simple вопрос: Will you go?

exercises.question: Выберите правильный порядок слов в Future Simple вопросе:
options: When will they arrive at the airport?
When they will arrive at the airport?
They will arrive when at the airport?
Will arrive they when at the airport?
exercises.explanation: Future Simple вопрос: Question word + Will + подлежащее + глагол?

videoQuiz1.text:
Look at the dialogue:
Peppa: In the future we will have a yellow car.
Suzy: No, blue.
Peppa: Yellow and blue.
Suzy: I will drive.
Peppa: So, will I.
Are they driving a yellow car right now?
videoQuiz1.explanation: They are talking about the future — the car does not exist yet. They are not driving right now.

videoQuiz1.text: Do Peppa and Suzy have the car now?
videoQuiz1.explanation: The car is a future plan. Peppa and Suzy do not have it now.

videoQuiz1.text: Did they have the car in the past?
videoQuiz1.explanation: The car is only a plan for the future — it did not exist in the past.

videoQuiz1.text: The car is real in the…
videoQuiz1.explanation: The car is a future dream — it will exist one day, but not now and not in the past.

videoQuiz1.text: Is this a plan for tomorrow morning, or for a long time from now?
videoQuiz1.explanation: Peppa says “in the future” — this is a dream about a distant future, not a specific plan for tomorrow.

videoQuiz1.text:
Look at the sentences:
Daddy dog will be a spaceman.
And George will be a dinosaur.
What auxiliary verb do we use to talk about the future?
videoQuiz1.explanation: We use will to talk about actions and events that will happen in the future.

videoQuiz1.text: Look at the main verbs in the sentences. Are they in infinitive form?
videoQuiz1.explanation: Yes — after will we always use the base (infinitive) form of the verb: will be, will drive, will have.

videoQuiz1.text:
Look at the sentences:
I will drive.
In the future we will have a yellow car.
Daddy will be a spaceman.
Does “will” change if the person changes?
videoQuiz1.explanation: The Future Simple auxiliary will is the same for all persons — I will, you will, he will, she will, we will, they will.

videoQuiz1.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz1.correct: Peppa Pig will have a yellow car.
videoQuiz1.explanation: The word order in the Future Simple is SAI (Subject + auxiliary verb + infinitive): Peppa Pig will have a yellow car.

grammarBank1.title: Future Simple +
grammarBank1.intro:
We use the Future Simple for future predictions, promises, and future plans.

Word order: SAI = Subject + will + infinitive
grammarBank1.structure:
The Future Simple auxiliary verb will is the same for all persons.

Iwill
Youwill
Hewill
Shewill
Itwill
Wewill
Theywill

Contractions: I’ll = I will  •  You’ll = You will  •  He’ll = He will  •  She’ll = She will  •  It’ll = It will  •  We’ll = We will  •  They’ll = They will

videoQuiz1b.text: In the future, Susie and Peppa _________ a car.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: After will we always use the infinitive (base form): will have.

videoQuiz1b.text: In the future, Peppa _________ a car.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: After will we always use the base form: will drive.
After will use the base form: will be.
After will use the base form: will be.
We need the auxiliary will before the infinitive to form the Future Simple.
We need the auxiliary will before the infinitive to form the Future Simple.

videoQuiz1b.text: They _________ the cars in the future.
videoQuiz1b.explanation: After will the verb stays in the base form: will drive — no -s or -ing.
After will use the base form: will be.
After will use the base form: will become — no -s or -ing.
After will use the base form: will be — not “is” or “are”.

videoQuiz2.text:
Look at the dialogue:
Rachel: What happened?
Phoebe: Hummus. I got the hummus.
Rachel: Honey, we will find you something. Do you want to wear my black jacket?
Phoebe: That won’t go with this dress, though.
Rachel: No, you are right. Well, we will find you something.
Did Rachel plan to find Phoebe something before she saw the stain on her dress?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
We use will + infinitive to talk about spontaneous decisions or quick reactions to something.
Examples: Someone knocked. I will open the door.   Stop shouting. He will cry.   They will stay. The weather is getting worse.

videoQuiz2.text: Finding Phoebe a new outfit — is it a long-term plan or a quick reaction to solve the problem?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Rachel makes a spontaneous decision in the moment. We use will for such quick reactions, not pre-planned arrangements.

videoQuiz2.text:
Look at the dialogue again:
Rachel: Honey, do you want to wear my black jacket?
Phoebe: That won’t go with this dress, though.
Rachel: No, you are right. Well, we will find you something.
When Phoebe says the black jacket won’t suit the dress — is she 100% sure, or is she predicting?
videoQuiz2.explanation:
We also use will + infinitive for predictions and promises.
Examples: I will be late, I guess.   I will help you with that later, I promise.

videoQuiz2.text: When Rachel says “we will find you something” — is she stating a fact or making a promise?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Will is used to make promises. Rachel is assuring Phoebe she will definitely help her.

videoQuiz2.text: Look at Phoebe’s line: It won’t go with this dress, though.
Is she giving a positive or a negative comment about the black jacket?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Won’t makes the sentence negative. Contraction: won’t = will + not.

videoQuiz2.text: What word is making the sentence negative?
videoQuiz2.explanation: Won’t = will + not. It is the negative form of will used in Future Simple.

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz2.explanation: Negative Future Simple word order: Subject + will not + infinitive: It will not rain today.

videoQuiz2.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz2.correct: The black jacket won't suit the yellow dress.
videoQuiz2.explanation: Negative Future Simple with contraction: Subject + won’t + infinitive: The black jacket won’t suit the yellow dress.

grammarBank2.title: Future Simple —
grammarBank2.intro:
We use will + infinitive to give promises, to state predictions, or when we make spontaneous decisions.

Word order for negative Future Simple: SAI = Subject + won’t + infinitive

Contraction: won’t = will + not
examples: He won’t be noisy anymore, he promises.
We won’t go outside, it started to rain.

videoQuiz2b.text: Practice — Usage & Negative: Choose the correct option.
Continue the sentence: “Someone is knocking at the door, _________.”
videoQuiz2b.explanation: We use will for spontaneous decisions made at the moment of speaking.

videoQuiz2b.text: Joey says “I’m really hungry.” How will you answer?
options: I will make you a sandwich
videoQuiz2b.explanation: A spontaneous reaction to what Joey just said — we use will for decisions made on the spot.

videoQuiz2b.text: The phone is ringing in the apartment.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Spontaneous decision in the moment: I will answer it.

videoQuiz2b.text: Someone spilled the water. Can somebody clean it?
options: I am going to clean it
videoQuiz2b.explanation: A spontaneous offer or decision: I will clean it.

videoQuiz2b.text: Finish the sentence: “I’m busy now, _________ later.”
videoQuiz2b.explanation: A promise made in the moment: I will call you later.

videoQuiz2b.text: Phoebe _________ her yellow dress to the event because it has a stain.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Negative Future Simple: Subject + will not + infinitive. The main verb stays in the base form: will not wear.

videoQuiz2b.text: Ross _________ calm if they don’t find a new outfit soon.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Will not be — after will not the verb stays in the base form: be, not being.

videoQuiz2b.text: Rachel _________ Phoebe wear a dirty dress to the party.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Negative: will not let. “Will no” and “not will” are not correct forms.

videoQuiz2b.text: Phoebe _________ the black jacket because it doesn’t match her dress.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Negative Future Simple: will not use. Only “will not” or “won’t” are correct.

videoQuiz2b.text: They _________ for the party until Phoebe is ready.
videoQuiz2b.explanation: Negative Future Simple: will not leave. “Will no” and “not will” are incorrect.

videoQuiz3.text:
Look at the dialogue:
Peppa: What will we do at the castle, Mummy?
Mama Pig: Let me see what the guidebook says.
Peppa: Will the castle be good or will it be boring?
Mamma Pig: It will be good.
Peppa: Will we see the knights in armour?

videoQuiz3.explanation:
The word order in interrogative Future Simple sentences is ASI (Auxiliary verb + Subject + Infinitive).
Examples: Will the castle be good?   Will the castle be boring?   Will we see the knights in armour?

videoQuiz3.text: The subject in the questions stays…
videoQuiz3.explanation: In Future Simple questions will comes first, then the subject: Will + subject + infinitive.

videoQuiz3.text:
Look at the dialogue:
Peppa: What will your job be?
Suzy: I will be a nurse. But in my lunch break I will fly a plane.
What will your job be, Peppa?
Peppa: My job will be doing important work on my computer.
What word starts the questions in this dialogue?
videoQuiz3.explanation:
What is a WH-question word. WH-question words stay at the beginning of the sentence.
Examples: What will your job be?   What will we do at the castle?

videoQuiz3.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz3.correct: Will Daddy Dog be a spaceman?
videoQuiz3.explanation: Yes/No question word order: Will + subject + infinitive? → Will Daddy Dog be a spaceman?

videoQuiz3.text: Put the words in the correct order:
videoQuiz3.correct: What will we do at school?
videoQuiz3.explanation: WH-question word order: WH-word + will + subject + infinitive? → What will we do at school?

blocks.intro:
INTERROGATIVE Future Simple
The word order for yes/no questions: Will + subject + infinitive?
examples: Will I see the dinosaurs at the zoo?
Will Peppa swim in the pool?
Will they go to watch the dolphins?

blocks.intro:
The WH-question words (who, what, why, when, where, etc.) stay at the beginning of interrogative sentences.
Word order: WH-word + will + subject + infinitive?
examples: What will George eat for lunch?
What will we do at the castle?

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ Suzy ______ a nurse when she grows up?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: Future Simple question: Will + subject + infinitive. The main verb stays in the base form: be.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ she ______ a plane during her lunch break?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: After will the verb is always in the base (infinitive) form: Will she fly.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ Peppa ______ a job on a computer?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: Will + subject + infinitive: Will Peppa have — no -s after will.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ they ______ in a house with fountains?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: Future Simple question: Will they live — base form, no -ing.
Will Peppa do — base form after will.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ the family ______ to a castle in their car?
______ Mummy Pig ______ the guidebook to see what to do?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: After will the verb stays in the base form: look, not looks.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ the castle ______ boring for Peppa and George?
videoQuiz3b.explanation: Will the castle be — base form be, not being.

videoQuiz3b.text: ______ they ______ knights in armour at the castle?

videoQuiz3b.explanation: Will Peppa hear — base form after will, no -s.
